stance: Says state government should live within its means, like Nevada households do.
Opposes legislation that would raise taxes; backs fair and transparent taxation.
quote: does not support legislation that will raise your taxes

stance: Supports school choice via charter schools, Education Savings Account vouchers, and Opportunity Scholarships, and rewarding effective teachers.
Says citizens must keep striving for more government transparency and hold elected officials accountable.
quote: continually strive for more transparency in government, and at all times hold our elected officials accountable

stance: Personally pro-life with exceptions for rape, incest, and life of the mother, but says Nevada voters settled the issue in state law.
Backs lowering the drug-possession threshold that triggers trafficking charges; lists public safety among his core priorities.
quote: lowering the threshold for drug possession for trafficking

stance: Conditional on the economics: she will only support data centers if the deal clearly pays off financially.
quote: In order for me to support them, it really has to pencil out.

stance: Not a fan of transferable tax credits, but would back incentives that generate strong ROI and revenue within 5 to 10 years.
quote: not a fan of transferable tax credits

stance: Skeptical of data centers, saying he struggles to view them as a net benefit.
quote: Data centers are hard for me to see as a net positive.

stance: Would grant tax incentives only with clear, measurable public benefit and claw-back provisions if promises aren't met.
quote: Tax incentives should only be considered when the public benefit is clear, measurable and enforceable

stance: Would focus spending on core services (public safety, education, infrastructure) and cut nonessential administrative growth and duplicative programs.
quote: focus on core services: public safety, education, critical infrastructure

stance: Says Nevadans are taxed enough and opposes 'Hollywood handouts' and large corporate tax breaks; favors relief for the general public.
quote: I don't see any need to raise taxes. I think that we're taxed enough.

stance: Opposes tax abatements for large companies, including data centers.
Wants to block housing development on toxic sites, citing mercury-contaminated areas.
quote: prevent housing developments on toxic sites, such as the mercury-contaminated areas

stance: Backs incentives as tools to attract businesses but opposed the film tax credit as a revenue loss, not an investment.
quote: good tools to try to get businesses to locate here

stance: Opposes resetting a home's taxable value on sale, saying it would worsen affordability; would examine depreciation allowances instead.
Supports housing tied to infrastructure: build in smart locations near existing roads, with developers funding traffic fixes and phased projects.
quote: We need housing, but not at the expense of quality of life. My idea is to tie growth to infrastructure.

stance: Emphasizes open, clear communication and early community input in city processes.
quote: open and clear communication and input from the community early on in these processes

stance: Names greater transparency, accountability, and accessibility among his top priorities alongside cost of living and city finances.
Wants to fix Nevada's property-tax system so cities aren't forced to chase any development just to fund basic services.
quote: cities aren't forced to chase any development just to fund basic services

stance: Proposes using Nevada Housing Access and Attainability Act grants to support housing.
